title: Cilium / Calico Kubernetes CNI Network Policy Enforcement, Pod-to-Pod Visibility, and eBPF Dataplane Health
exclusions: Traditional SDN controllers running on bare-metal Linux (use UC-18.3.3 — different control-plane); physical-fabric VXLAN/EVPN dataplane (use UC-18.3.4 — physical leaf-spine fabric, not K8s); Cisco ACI integration with K8s (use UC-18.1.* — APIC manages the network, K8s CNI is overlay); VMware NSX-T container plugin (NCP) for K8s (use UC-18.2.* — NSX-T extends DFW into K8s); Antrea CNI (use cat-12 if relevant — different open-source K8s CNI); Multus CNI multi-network attach (use cat-12 if relevant); legacy Flannel CNI (out-of-scope; minimal NetworkPolicy support).
dataSources: `index=k8s_network` / `index=kubernetes_ops` / `index=container_platform` / `index=os` (rename per governance) combining **`sourcetype=cilium:hubble:flow`** (flattened Hubble OTLP or HEC JSON with **verdict**, **policy_match_type**, **source.namespace**, **destination.namespace**, **l7.HTTP.method**, **drop_reason_desc**); **`sourcetype=calico:felix:flow`** (Calico Cloud or Whisker summaries with **action**, **source_namespace**, **dest_name_aggr**, **policies**, **reporter**); **`sourcetype=cilium:agent:log`** / **`sourcetype=calico:felix:log`** pod logs via Splunk Connect for Kubernetes; optional **`sourcetype=kube:audit`** / **`kube:container:log`** for NetworkPolicy CRD edits—parallel HEC lanes kept distinct so cardinality spikes do not starve agent-health EPS budgets.
app: **Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes** ([splunk-otel-collector-chart](https://github.com/signalfx/splunk-otel-collector-chart)) with OTLP receivers feeding Splunk Platform exporters; **Splunk Connect for Kubernetes** ([documentation](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-connect-for-kubernetes/)) for DaemonSet log forwarding; optional **Splunk App for Infrastructure** for fleet CPU or RAM overlays on cilium and calico DaemonSets.
spl:
(index=k8s_network OR index=kubernetes_ops OR index=container_platform OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cilium:hubble:flow" OR sourcetype="calico:felix:flow")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval verdict_u=upper(trim(coalesce(verdict, "", "")))
| eval action_u=upper(trim(coalesce(action, "", "")))
| eval flow_result=case(
    verdict_u IN ("ALLOWED","DROPPED","ERROR","AUDIT"), verdict_u,
    action_u IN ("ALLOW","DENY","PASS"), action_u,
    1=1, coalesce(verdict_u, action_u, "UNKNOWN"))
| eval src_ns=trim(coalesce('source.namespace', source_namespace, ""))
| eval dst_ns=trim(coalesce('destination.namespace', dest_namespace, ""))
| eval pm_type=trim(coalesce(policy_match_type, "", ""))
| eval http_method=trim(coalesce('l7.HTTP.method', "", ""))
| eval l7_kind=trim(coalesce('l7.type', "", ""))
| eval drop_hint=trim(coalesce(drop_reason_desc, "", ""))
| eval dest_pod_agg=trim(coalesce(dest_name_aggr, 'destination.pod_name', ""))
| eval reporter_side=lower(trim(coalesce(reporter, "", "")))
| eval node_hint=trim(coalesce(node_name, host, ""))
| eval notable=case(
    flow_result IN ("DROPPED","DENY"), "explicit_deny",
    flow_result="ERROR", "error_or_fail_close",
    flow_result="AUDIT", "audit_trail_only",
    match(lower(drop_hint), "(?i)(identity|endpoint|cilium)"), "identity_or_endpoint_string",
    pm_type IN ("L7","L3_L4") AND flow_result IN ("ALLOWED","ALLOW"), "policy_matched_allow",
    1=1, null())
| where isnotnull(notable)
| stats count as flow_events,
    dc(src_ns) as src_ns_diversity,
    dc(dst_ns) as dst_ns_diversity,
    values(pm_type) as policy_match_types,
    values(http_method) as http_verbs_seen,
    values(l7_kind) as l7_kinds_seen,
    latest(flow_result) as verdict_latest,
    latest(reporter_side) as reporter_latest,
    latest(node_hint) as node_latest
  by notable, src_ns, dst_ns, dest_pod_agg
| eval traffic_pair=strcat(if(isnull(src_ns) OR src_ns="", "(none)", src_ns), " -> ", if(isnull(dst_ns) OR dst_ns="", "(none)", dst_ns))
| sort -flow_events
| head 300
| table notable traffic_pair dest_pod_agg flow_events policy_match_types http_verbs_seen l7_kinds_seen verdict_latest reporter_latest node_latest
description: Monitors Kubernetes CNI dataplane posture for Cilium 1.16 cilium-agent DaemonSets, Hubble flow export, eBPF TC hooks plus optional XDP, CiliumNetworkPolicy and CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy CRDs, CiliumIdentity and CiliumEndpoint lifecycle signals, and Calico 3.28 Felix with optional Typha, GlobalNetworkPolicy tier ordering, iptables versus BPF dataplanes—correlating NetworkPolicy intent with verdict or action outcomes, Layer-7 policy_match_type cues, Container Egress Gateway SNAT shifts, sidecar-less Cilium Service Mesh pairings or Istio sidecars atop Calico, and agent fault narratives distinct from physical fabric overlays monitored under sibling data-center UC entries.
value: When pod labels drift faster than ticket queues refresh, deny bursts surface inside namespaces before ICMP probes notice—folding Hubble DROPPED or AUDIT rows, Calico Deny or Pass semantics, policy_match_type hints, Felix restart timelines, Cilium Operator leadership cues, and Typha saturation signals into Splunk collapses redundant bridges across platform SREs, security engineers, and RBAC owners chasing unrelated kube-apiserver saturation alarms.
implementation: Deploy Splunk OTel Kubernetes collectors or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes for cilium:hubble:flow, calico:felix:flow, cilium:agent:log, calico:felix:log; normalize FIELDALIAS mappings for source.namespace versus flattened keys; schedule macros wrapping the spl payload with governance-approved index names; attach optional kube:audit lookups when correlating CRD edits with lateral movement hypotheses.
visualization: (1) Timechart of flow_events by notable bucket. (2) Heatmap src_ns × dst_ns deny density. (3) Table traffic_pair, policy_match_types, verdict_latest for ticketing. (4) Single value ratio explicit_denies versus total flows. (5) Drilldown node_latest distribution for BPF reload investigations paired with cilium status timelines.
schema: CIM: Network_Traffic (pod-to-pod src or dest tuples, verdict or action outcomes, bytes counters where extracted) plus Authentication (identity-centric policy selectors—CiliumIdentity labels and ServiceAccount-driven selectors mirrored into flow dimensions).
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Network_Traffic once stable src_ns, dst_ns, and transport keys exist via tstats from normalized aliases; Authentication overlays help when identity or policy tier fields align to CIM user or app pivots without thrashing summaries.
knownFalsePositives:
**Helm rollout deny bursts** — rolling Pod restarts legitimately churn CiliumEndpoint programming such that DROPPED spikes appear briefly while cilium-agent reattaches eBPF programs—suppress against CHG tickets referencing helm revision bumps plus Rollout pause annotations rather than paging raw deny velocity alone.

**Identity garbage-collection timing** — CiliumIdentity reclamation lagging behind Deployment scale-down windows yields identity-oriented drop_reason_desc strings resembling breaches though Namespace membership remains compliant—cross-check cilium identity list snapshots beside Splunk timelines before escalation.

**Felix iptables reload pauses** — Felix iptables dataplane briefly halts enforcement during in-place upgrades whereas BPF dataplane avoids iptables-save restore stalls—Splunk calico:felix:log WARN mixes differ by mode; verify FelixConfiguration before blaming Deny telemetry during upgrades.

**Hubble OTLP saturation** — Hubble exporters constrained on high-flow clusters drop spans when OpenTelemetry batch queues back up—Splunk gaps resemble healthy silence; watch collector splunk exporter retry metrics and memory_limiter settings tied to DaemonSet sizing.

**ClusterPool IPAM exhaustion** — cluster-pool CIDR carve-outs filling under bursty autoscaling resemble pod scheduling failures logged as no-IP style errors inside cilium:agent:log—pair Splunk ERROR lanes with cilium ip diagnostics before attributing denies to NetworkPolicy mistakes.

**Calico overlay MTU edge cases** — VXLAN or IPIP encapsulation shrinks effective MTU; only jumbo-sized payloads fail while routine probes Allow—correlate Deny absence on small pings with interface mtu counters on tunl0 or vxlan.calico devices when large-object transfers fail.
hardware: Cilium and Calico Kubernetes CNIs deploy as DaemonSets running in Linux container runtimes (containerd 1.7+, CRI-O 1.30+, Docker 24+) on Linux x86_64 / ARM64 worker nodes (RHEL 8/9, Ubuntu 22.04 / 24.04, CentOS Stream 9, Debian 12, Bottlerocket 1.x, Talos 1.x). Kubernetes versions: 1.27, 1.28, 1.29, 1.30, 1.31 (current Apr 2026); CNI bridge requires Linux kernel 5.4+ for stable eBPF (kernel 5.10+ recommended for Cilium 1.16 advanced features like XDP, BPF-LSM, sockmap acceleration). Worker-node sizing: 4-32 vCPU / 8-128 GB RAM typical; CNI agent overhead is ~50-200 MB RAM per node. Hubble Relay deployment: 1-3 replicas for high-availability flow-log aggregation. Calico Typha proxy: 3-5 replicas for clusters with 200+ nodes.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• Pin inventories to **Cilium 1.15–1.16** DaemonSets for **cilium-agent** with **Hubble** enabled; confirm kube-proxy replacement mode versus hybrid iptables rollouts documented in your cluster baseline.
• Pin **Calico 3.27–3.28** **Felix** DaemonSets plus optional **Typha**; record whether **FelixConfiguration** selects iptables, BPF, or hybrid dataplane because Splunk fingerprints differ during troubleshooting.
• Deploy Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes ([splunk-otel-collector-chart](https://github.com/signalfx/splunk-otel-collector-chart)) to receive **Hubble OTLP** telemetry, or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes tailing cilium-agent and calico-node pods into **cilium:agent:log** and **calico:felix:log**; land normalized flows on **cilium:hubble:flow** and **calico:felix:flow** with distinct **HEC** tokens per environment.
• Map Kubernetes **ServiceAccount** tokens used by collectors; rotate **HEC** tokens independently from kubectl credentials; restrict **kube:audit** readers when pairing audit denies with flow denies for investigations.
• Prefer flattened dotted keys such as **source.namespace** where OTLP exporters preserve structure; mirror **destination.namespace** versus dest_namespace variants via **FIELDALIAS** in props.conf until teams converge.

**Step 1 — Land pod-to-pod evidence across CNIs**
(1) **Cilium Hubble flows** — Enable **Hubble Relay** replicas (1–3 typical) so **cilium:hubble:flow** carries **verdict** values ALLOWED/DROPPED/ERROR/AUDIT plus **policy_match_type** markers such as **L3_L4**, **L7**, KNOWN_TRAFFIC, CUSTOM_REASON; retain **drop_reason_desc**, **source.identity** and **destination.identity**, l7.HTTP.method/code/type fields, **node_name**, and **IP.encrypted** hints for WireGuard or IPsec encapsulation.
(2) **Calico flow logs** — When Calico Cloud or Whisker publishes flow rows, ingest action Allow/Deny/Pass alongside **source_namespace** and **dest_namespace**, **source_name_aggr** and **dest_name_aggr**, policies payloads, reporter src versus dst into **calico:felix:flow** without leaning on leaf-switch syslog alone.
(3) **Agent health logs** — Mirror **cilium:agent:log** and **calico:felix:log** kubectl logs streams via DaemonSet collectors to expose **BPF** map pressure, Felix iptables reload loops, and **Typha** connection backoff distinct from dataplane verdict churn.
(4) **Kubernetes audit** — Optional **kube:audit** subset capturing networking.k8s.io/networkpolicies plus cilium.io and projectcalico.org CRD writes so intent changes pair with **flow_result** deltas inside Splunk drills.
(5) **Egress attribution** — Track **cilium.io/v2alpha1.CiliumEgressGatewayPolicy**-driven SNAT shifts alongside Calico egress controls so **Container Egress Gateway** migrations do not masquerade as lateral movement when **destination.pod_name** disappears behind NAT edges.

**Step 2 — Normalize identities and publish macros**
(1) Build saved-search **macros** wrapping index and sourcetype unions so dashboards stay aligned when governance splits prod versus nonprod indexes.
(2) **FIELDALIAS** **src_ns** from **source.namespace** or **source_namespace** after validating **fieldsummary** across exporters—avoid silent blank stats splits during Helm upgrades renaming OTLP attributes.
(3) **lookup** **k8s_namespace_owner** CSV or KVStore enriching team and tier tags—helps route **DROPPED** or Deny bursts without paging bare namespace strings alone.
(4) Pipeline tuning distinguishes **AUDIT** markers from **ERROR** dataplane faults—both merit investigation yet imply different operator responses than ALLOWED or Allow steady state.
(5) Version-control SPL edits in Git—annotate tweaks whenever **Cilium 1.16** exporters add **event_type.sub_type** granularity affecting **where** predicates.

**Step 3 — Install saved searches using the SPL below**

```spl
(index=k8s_network OR index=kubernetes_ops OR index=container_platform OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cilium:hubble:flow" OR sourcetype="calico:felix:flow")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval verdict_u=upper(trim(coalesce(verdict, "", "")))
| eval action_u=upper(trim(coalesce(action, "", "")))
| eval flow_result=case(
    verdict_u IN ("ALLOWED","DROPPED","ERROR","AUDIT"), verdict_u,
    action_u IN ("ALLOW","DENY","PASS"), action_u,
    1=1, coalesce(verdict_u, action_u, "UNKNOWN"))
| eval src_ns=trim(coalesce('source.namespace', source_namespace, ""))
| eval dst_ns=trim(coalesce('destination.namespace', dest_namespace, ""))
| eval pm_type=trim(coalesce(policy_match_type, "", ""))
| eval http_method=trim(coalesce('l7.HTTP.method', "", ""))
| eval l7_kind=trim(coalesce('l7.type', "", ""))
| eval drop_hint=trim(coalesce(drop_reason_desc, "", ""))
| eval dest_pod_agg=trim(coalesce(dest_name_aggr, 'destination.pod_name', ""))
| eval reporter_side=lower(trim(coalesce(reporter, "", "")))
| eval node_hint=trim(coalesce(node_name, host, ""))
| eval notable=case(
    flow_result IN ("DROPPED","DENY"), "explicit_deny",
    flow_result="ERROR", "error_or_fail_close",
    flow_result="AUDIT", "audit_trail_only",
    match(lower(drop_hint), "(?i)(identity|endpoint|cilium)"), "identity_or_endpoint_string",
    pm_type IN ("L7","L3_L4") AND flow_result IN ("ALLOWED","ALLOW"), "policy_matched_allow",
    1=1, null())
| where isnotnull(notable)
| stats count as flow_events,
    dc(src_ns) as src_ns_diversity,
    dc(dst_ns) as dst_ns_diversity,
    values(pm_type) as policy_match_types,
    values(http_method) as http_verbs_seen,
    values(l7_kind) as l7_kinds_seen,
    latest(flow_result) as verdict_latest,
    latest(reporter_side) as reporter_latest,
    latest(node_hint) as node_latest
  by notable, src_ns, dst_ns, dest_pod_agg
| eval traffic_pair=strcat(if(isnull(src_ns) OR src_ns="", "(none)", src_ns), " -> ", if(isnull(dst_ns) OR dst_ns="", "(none)", dst_ns))
| sort -flow_events
| head 300
| table notable traffic_pair dest_pod_agg flow_events policy_match_types http_verbs_seen l7_kinds_seen verdict_latest reporter_latest node_latest
```

**Search mechanics**
• **flow_result** harmonizes Cilium verdict tokens with Calico action verbs so one stats spine works across mixed CNI estates during migrations.
• notable buckets separate operator-urgent explicit_deny rows from audit_trail_only AUDIT markers—triage avoids chasing AUDIT spikes during blanket auditing intervals unless ERROR accompanies them.
• **policy_match_types** aggregates **policy_match_type** values (L7 versus L3_L4)—when http_verbs_seen populates alongside l7_kinds_seen, analysts recognize **CiliumNetworkPolicy** Layer-7 posture versus kubernetes.io NetworkPolicy alone.
• **dest_pod_agg** prefers **dest_name_aggr** aggregation-friendly labels Calico emits—pairs with traffic_pair even when ephemeral pod_name churn obscures raw identities.

**Step 4 — Validate alongside Kubernetes consoles**
(1) Pick explicit_deny rows—confirm kubectl NetworkPolicy and CRD objects cited in kube:audit near _time align with Splunk denies; mismatches imply stale flow export or Hubble sampling gaps.
(2) Compare node_latest against kubectl get pod -n kube-system -o wide locating cilium-agent or calico-node—if Splunk lacks a node while flow_events continue, host versus node_name alias drift exists.
(3) cilium-dbg or kubectl exec into cilium-agent validates whether drop_reason_desc phrases map to BPF TC hooks versus XDP paths when ERROR dominates.
(4) calicoctl get GlobalNetworkPolicy—Tier ordering explains Pass rows that bypass lower-priority NetworkPolicy expectations—do not treat Pass as always benign without tier context.
(5) stern or kubectl logs spot checks echo cilium:agent:log ERROR streaks Splunk already shows—confirms indexer lag versus genuine agent faults.

**Step 5 — Operational misreads and recovery paths**
• Empty notable rows yet widespread outage — widen sourcetype list to **cilium:agent:log** ERROR lanes; **Hubble** may be disabled while **cilium** dataplane still drops—flow absence is not green health.
• **flow_events** balloon only on one **src_ns** — investigate **CiliumIdentity** label churn or Namespace label edits; identity relabels ripple through **eBPF** maps attached at TC ingress and egress on pod **veth** pairs.
• ERROR verdicts during **Cilium** upgrades — correlate with **DaemonSet** rollout **minReadySeconds**; extend suppress windows using change tickets tied to cluster UID hashes stamped in CMDB.
• **policy_match_types** blank while **http_verbs_seen** populated — OTLP flattening renamed fields—refresh **FIELDALIAS** after exporter chart bumps ship new attribute paths.
• **reporter_latest** skew src versus dst on same pair — asymmetric routes across Geneve or **VXLAN** overlays inside Kubernetes may duplicate flows—dedupe using five-tuple hashes when exporters expose them.
• **dest_pod_agg** generic — **dest_name_aggr** rolling patterns hide individual microservice pods—acceptable for capacity baselines yet pair with **kube:audit** when forensics demands exact pod identities.
• Spikes in **audit_trail_only** without security stories — compliance scans toggled AUDIT verbosity—suppress using CMDB **policy_baseline** tokens referencing quarterly attestations.
• **node_latest** flaps — node rename or cloud instance reuse in autoscaling—carry **kubelet** **providerID** through sidecar enrichment to stabilize entity keys Splunk dashboards group by.
• **Calico** Pass storms — **Tier** Order edits during GitOps merges—confirm **Argo CD** diffs before blaming malware east-west probes inside namespaces.
• **Multus** secondary interfaces — duplicate events from extra pod NICs may double counts—tag **network_attachment** metadata when Splunk Connect for Kubernetes enriches DaemonSet payloads.
• OTLP queue drops — OpenTelemetry exporter retries appear as intermittent flow gaps distinct from **DROPPED** verdict semantics—watch collector internal metrics dashboards bundled with **splunk-otel-collector-chart**.
• Splunk ingest lag — heavy forwarder congestion mimics realtime deny—compare **_indextime** minus **_time** deltas per sourcetype before blaming **eBPF** dataplanes or **Felix** alone.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the plug-ins on each Linux worker that stitch pods into the cluster network and enforce who may talk to whom, using the traffic notes they emit. When enforcement glitches or those helpers weaken, we speak up early so folks adjust Kubernetes namespace rules or fix the agents before quiet breakage spreads.
